title: Parallel Tool Calls, TaskGroup Ownership, Ordering, and Shared Effects · Transfer Build
summary: An independent organization supplies a different user population, task distribution, tool set, risk tier, model response, resource budget, accessibility need, and failure condition and transfers parallel tool calls, taskgroup ownership, ordering, and shared effects into a changed structured bounded parallel tool executor. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of structured bounded parallel tool executor with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through p…
objectives: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.
Own concurrent calls with asyncio TaskGroup, explicit timeout and semaphore budgets, and structured error and cancellation propagation.
Correlate each result by call ID and return required result parts in provider-valid order independent of completion order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Call Independence
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab agent engineering team handles case 2677 during a multilingual case-routing evaluation. Build deterministic Python evidence to run independent tools with bounded TaskGroup ownership, stable call-result correlation, serialized shared effects, cancellation, and cleanup; telemetry and memory must remain tenant-isolated, minimized, redacted, and attributable; then inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects. Browser code uses pure bounded state machines and original fixed fixtures only. Real provider APIs, Gemini models, google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic native validation, HTTP, files, credentials, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, privacy review, and production effects require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove tool-call independence from inputs, effects, authority, ordering, rate limits, shared resources, and compensating behavior before parallel execution.

steps.title: Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Parallel Cleanup
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: agentpy-parallel-cleanup-199ul8" that uses case 8121 to run independent tools with bounded TaskGroup ownership, stable call-result correlation, serialized shared effects, cancellation, and cleanup. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not contact provider APIs, invoke Gemini models, import or claim native google-genai, google-adk, or Pydantic behavior, open sockets, read or write host files, use credentials, execute generated code, launch browsers, reach databases or telemetry backends, or claim live privacy, load, safety, cost, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, google-genai 2.11.0, google-adk 2.4.0, Pydantic 2.13.4, fixed provider fixtures, fake transports, controlled local services, disposable cloud projects, representative eval sets, accessibility, privacy and security review, load, canary, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Parallel Cleanup into observable performance evidence. For Safe AI agents and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Parallel Cleanup, keep Parallel Cleanup connected to executable Python model, interaction, tool, policy, state, approval, evaluation, security, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-parallel-cleanup-199ul8
# Competency: agentpy-parallel-cleanup.
# Case 8121: a clean package, canary, and recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: inject cancellation, retry, repeated-call, checkpoint, or replay failure and recover without duplicate effects.
def evidence_agentpy_parallel_cleanup_199ul8(scheduled_calls: int = 12, completed_calls: int = 12, parallel_limit: int = 4, peak_parallel: int = 3, leaked_tasks: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_199ul8 = "199ul8-8lu991-8121"
    assert evidence_variant_199ul8
    evidence_axes_199ul8 = (
        "authority199ul8",
        "interaction8121",
        "tool8lu991",
        "state199ul88121",
        "evaluation8121199ul8",
        "recovery8lu9918121",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_199ul8)) == 6
    if scheduled_calls < 0 or completed_calls < 0:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-count"
    if completed_calls != scheduled_calls:
        return False, completed_calls, "parallel-reconciliation"
    if parallel_limit < 1 or peak_parallel > parallel_limit:
        return False, peak_parallel, "parallel-budget"
    if leaked_tasks > 0:
        return False, leaked_tasks, "task-leak"
    assert completed_calls == scheduled_calls and leaked_tasks == 0
    return True, completed_calls, "structured-tool-concurrency"
